titleStmt.title: The Chargé in Egypt ( Patterson ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Chargé in Egypt ( Patterson ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 907. Information received by IrelandPhilip W. Ireland, First Secretary of Embassy in Egypt. through discreet conversations with Bourguiba indicates (1) Bourguiba and Abd El KarimMohammed Ben Emir Abd-el Krim, Moroccan Rif leader who escaped at Port Said from long-time exile while en route from Reunion to France in early June. continue dominate local North African nationalists; (2) they agree North Africans will not move toward opening Maghreb office America or appealing UN until return Azzam Pasha here; (3) Bourguiba holds presentations NA case to UN not probable until 1948; (4) Abd El Karim does not intend visit US soon (Deptel 1055, July 17, Embtel 899, July 28Neither printed.); (5) Bourguiba will visit America only in accordance advice Azzam Pasha but privately wishes visit October; (6) plans Torres and Al FassiAbdul Khalek Torres, president of the Spanish Moroccan Reform Party, and Si Allal El Fassi, leader of Moroccan Istiqlal Party. visit US cancelled (Embtel 889, July 25Not printed.); (7) arrival Moncef BeyMoncef had been deposed in May 1943 and interned in France. in Egypt cannot be ruled out. Memo conversations by pouch.None printed.
